search_terms_used: training zone systems comparison 5 zone 3 zone heart rate lactate endurance PubMed
training intensity distribution zone systems running Seiler Sports Medicine
heart rate zone validity endurance training prescription lactate threshold PubMed
5 zone vs 3 zone training intensity distribution running cycling
lactate zone training prescription training zones comparison validity systematic review meta-analysis PubMed
polarized training versus threshold training meta-analysis systematic review endurance athletes 2024 Sports Medicine PubMed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage across the full spectrum: foundational descriptive work (Seiler 2006, Seiler 2010), methodological comparison of zone quantification methods (Sylta 2014), systematic reviews and meta-analyses comparing training intensity distributions (Stoggl 2014, Stoggl 2015, Rosenblat 2019, Oliveira 2024), an RCT directly comparing pyramidal vs polarized in runners (Filipas 2022), and contemporary papers addressing zone definition problems (Meixner 2025, Sitko 2025, Seiler-Viken 2025, Sun 2025). Populations include elite cross-country skiers, well-trained cyclists, recreational and well-trained runners, and mixed endurance athletes. Gap: no paper specifically validates the Daniels 5-zone or Friel 7-zone system in isolation with a controlled design -- these systems are practitioner-derived rather than experimentally validated as distinct frameworks. The 3-zone Seiler model has the strongest empirical basis. Running-specific RCTs are represented (Filipas 2022) but most meta-analyses pool across endurance sports.

papers.title: Quantifying training intensity distribution in elite endurance athletes: is there evidence for an "optimal" distribution?
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine & Science in Sports
papers.notes: Quantified daily training intensity distribution in junior cross-country skiers using three methods (heart rate time-in-zone, session goal, session RPE) across a 3-zone model based on VT1/VT2.

papers.title: What is best practice for training intensity and duration distribution in endurance athletes?
papers.journal: International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance
papers.notes: Narrative review synthesizing descriptive studies on training intensity distribution in elite endurance athletes, proposing the ~80/20 low-to-high intensity distribution model.

papers.title: From heart-rate data to training quantification: a comparison of 3 methods of training-intensity analysis
papers.journal: International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance
papers.notes: Compared three HR-based training intensity quantification methods (time-in-zone, session goal, hybrid) across 570 sessions in elite cross-country skiers using 3-zone and 5-zone models.

papers.title: Polarized training has greater impact on key endurance variables than threshold, high intensity, or high volume training
papers.notes: RCT comparing 9 weeks of four training intensity distributions (polarized, threshold, high-intensity, high-volume) in trained endurance athletes across running, cycling, and triathlon.

papers.title: The training intensity distribution among well-trained and elite endurance athletes
papers.notes: Systematic review examining training intensity distribution patterns in well-trained and elite endurance athletes across multiple sports, comparing polarized, pyramidal, threshold, and high-volume models.

papers.title: Polarized vs. Threshold Training Intensity Distribution on Endurance Sport Performance: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs comparing polarized versus threshold training intensity distributions on endurance sport performance outcomes.

papers.title: Effects of 16 weeks of pyramidal and polarized training intensity distributions in well-trained endurance runners
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine & Science in Sports
papers.notes: RCT comparing four 16-week periodization strategies (pyramidal, polarized, pyramidal-then-polarized, polarized-then-pyramidal) in well-trained male runners on VO2max, lactate thresholds, and 5 km performance.

papers.title: Comparison of Polarized Versus Other Types of Endurance Training Intensity Distribution on Athletes' Endurance Performance: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis
papers.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 17 studies comparing polarized training to other intensity distributions on VO2peak, time-trial, time-to-exhaustion, and threshold outcomes in endurance athletes.

papers.title: Zone 2 Intensity: A Critical Comparison of Individual Variability in Different Submaximal Exercise Intensity Boundaries
papers.notes: Compared individual variability in Zone 2 boundaries across different physiological markers (VT1, FatMax, %HRmax, blood lactate) in 50 cyclists, finding coefficients of variation from 6-29%.

papers.title: What Is "Zone 2 Training"?: Experts' Viewpoint on Definition, Training Methods, and Expected Adaptations
papers.journal: International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance
papers.notes: Expert consensus statement from 14 sport scientists defining Zone 2 training as intensities below the first lactate/ventilatory threshold, addressing inconsistencies in how the term is used across coaching and research.

papers.title: Contextualizing the Norwegian standardized intensity zone framework in an international sample of endurance practitioners
papers.notes: Survey of 778 international endurance practitioners comparing zone scale preferences (3/5/7-zone) and HR-based zone demarcations across regions and sports, finding Norwegians 2.7x more likely to use a 5-zone scale.

papers.title: Recent advances in training intensity distribution theory for cyclic endurance sports: theoretical foundations, model comparisons, and periodization characteristics
papers.notes: Narrative review comparing theoretical foundations and periodization characteristics of polarized, pyramidal, threshold, and block training intensity distribution models in cyclic endurance sports.
